meta.originalQuestion: Synthesize pilot v1/v2 findings and paper.md into a run-ready v3 experimental specification mapped to current engine settings, ablations, canary gates, telemetry coverage, and decision criteria.

areasOfAnalysis.name: Pilot v2 geometry and unresolved ablations

assignments.task: Extract v1 empirical findings and confounds that directly affect v3 protocol design.
Extract v2 geometry findings and unresolved ablations to prioritize in v3.
Extract paper-level hypotheses and metrics to preserve in v3.
Extract current engine/runbook constraints so v3 is runnable now.

microHypotheses.statement: Compression-like geometry is reproducible in GPT-5.x variants under current prompting and can be quantified with dynamic-range metrics.
Forced-choice inflation and framework sensitivity are plausible, but currently under-validated and must be front-loaded as ablations before large scale sweeps.
A staged canary pipeline with strict guardrails is sufficient to run v3 safely in current infrastructure.

hypothesisConflicts.conflict: Hypothetical framing may reduce refusals without reducing compression; effect size is undefined.
Subset verdicts were weak in prior pilot due abstention dominance, so forced-choice inflation magnitude is uncertain.
